extra.reasoning:
The monotonicity property requires that flipping syncope from false to true (all other inputs fixed) never lowers acuity. It is violated.

syncope appears in only two ways in the rule set: (1) as one of the eight required conjuncts of the interaction_drop rule (priority 100, sets acuity := self_care, the LOWEST acuity), and (2) nowhere else — there is no raise_syncope rule. Thus enabling syncope can only newly enable the acuity-lowering interaction_drop rule, which is the textbook monotonicity-violation pattern.

Counterexample comparison with all other inputs fixed at hemoptysis=true, confusion=true, dyspnea=true, focal_weakness=true, stiff_neck=true, chest_pain=true, abdominal_pain=true, vision_loss=false:

- syncope = FALSE (baseline): interaction_drop does NOT fire (it requires syncope). The highest-priority matching rules are raise_chest_pain / raise_focal_weakness / raise_confusion, all priority 3, each setting acuity := emergent. Result = emergent.

- syncope = TRUE (witness, shown): now all eight conjuncts of interaction_drop are satisfied, so it fires at priority 100, which exceeds the priority-3 raise rules, so it wins under per-output priority conflict resolution: acuity := self_care. Result = self_care.

Flipping syncope false->true lowers acuity from emergent to self_care (self_care < routine < urgent < emergent). This violates monotonicity. The witness_assignment is the syncope=true configuration that yields the lower (self_care) outcome.
